stem: Each row below describes an action. Tick the UK law that the action most directly breaks.
model_answer: {"Logging in to a friend's school account using their password without permission": "Computer Misuse Act 1990", "Downloading the latest film from a torrent site for free": "Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988", "A company keeping pupil contact details forever after the pupil leaves": "Data Protection Act 2018 / UK GDPR", "Writing a virus that wipes hard drives in a school's network": "Computer Misuse Act 1990"}
review_notes: One tick per row. Common pupil confusion: copyright vs data protection when files contain personal data.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Tick the law that each action most directly breaks. One tick per row.

rows: Logging in to a friend's school account using their password without permission
Downloading the latest film from a torrent site for free
A company keeping pupil contact details forever after the pupil leaves
Writing a virus that wipes hard drives in a school's network

mark_points.text: illegal download — Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988
indefinite retention of personal data — Data Protection Act 2018 / UK GDPR
